benefitsPage.title: Your Benefits at a Glance
benefitsPage.description: Explore the complete range of benefits available to Premier America Credit Union employees.

benefitChapters.title: Overview of Available Plans
benefitChapters.description: Overview of all available benefit plans for Premier America Credit Union employees.

children.text: Review and compare all benefit plans available to Premier America Credit Union employees.
Medical Plans

Medical benefits are provided through Anthem Blue Cross and include coverage for both in-network and non-network providers. The plan is an HDHP with preventive care often covered at 100% in-network; out-of-network services are subject to Reasonable and Customary limitations. The medical plan premium contributions are deducted from your paycheck on a pre-tax basis.
1 medical plan available: HDHP
Dental Plans
Dental plan is provided by Anthem Blue Cross and emphasizes staying in-network to reduce out-of-pocket costs and avoid charges beyond Reasonable and Customary amounts.
1 dental plan available: ANTHEM BLUE CROSS - PPO
Vision Plans
Vision benefits are provided through EyeMed with coverage for in-network and non-network providers. Premium contributions are deducted from your paycheck on a pre-tax basis.
1 vision plan available: INSIGHT - PPO

tables.tableDescription: Bi-weekly associate payroll contributions for medical coverage.
Bi-weekly associate payroll contributions for dental coverage.
Bi-weekly associate payroll contributions for vision coverage.

benefitChapters.title: Eligibility & Qualifying Life Events
benefitChapters.description: Information about who is eligible for benefits, eligible dependents, enrollment, and qualifying life events.

children.text: Premier America Credit Union’s benefits are designed to support your unique needs.
Eligibility
Premier America Credit Union’s benefits are designed to support your unique needs.
You must be a full-time team member regularly scheduled to work at least 20 hours per week on a continual basis (excluding internal and external temporary staff).
You become eligible to participate in medical, dental, vision, life and disability plans, and additional benefits.
Eligible Dependents
Your legal spouse/domestic partner.

Children under the age of 26, including birth children, stepchildren, legally adopted children, children placed for adoption, foster children, and children for whom you or your spouse/domestic partner have legal guardianship.

Dependent children age 26 or older who are unmarried and primarily supported by you, incapable of self-sustaining employment due to mental or physical disability originating while covered as a dependent under this plan.
Enrollment
Your elections are effective the first day of the month following the date of hire.
Benefits cannot be changed until the next enrollment period unless you experience a Qualifying Life Event.
Common Qualifying Events
Qualifying Life Events (QLEs) allow benefit changes outside of open enrollment periods based on certain life changes.
You have 30 days to request benefit changes after a Qualifying Life Event. Changes must be consistent with your change in status.
Change in number of dependents through birth or adoption or loss of dependent eligibility.
Change in legal marital status (marriage, divorce, legal separation).
Change in a spouse/domestic partner’s employment status (gain or loss of coverage).
Entitlement to Medicare or Medicaid.
Eligibility for coverage through the Marketplace (Healthcare.gov).
Changes in address or location that affect coverage.
Turning 26 and losing coverage through a parent’s plan.
Lesser-Known Qualifying Events
Change in employment status from full-time to part-time or vice versa resulting in gain or loss of eligibility.
Death in the family leading to change in dependents or loss of coverage.
Changes causing ineligibility for Medicaid or the Children’s Health Insurance Program (CHIP).

benefitChapters.title: HDHP Medical Plan
benefitChapters.description: Premier America Credit Union offers the HDHP medical plan through Anthem Blue Cross with in-network and out-of-network coverage. Preventive care is covered at no charge in-network. The plan has a dedu

children.text: Premier America Credit Union offers the HDHP medical plan through Anthem Blue Cross with in-network and out-of-network coverage. Preventive care is covered at no charge in-network. The plan has a deductible, coinsurance, and out-of-pocket maximum thresholds which must be met.
Copays
A fixed amount you pay for healthcare services at the time you receive them.
Deductible
You must pay a certain amount for covered services before your insurance begins paying its portion.
Individual deductible is $1,650 in-network and $4,950 out-of-network.
Family deductible is $4,100 ($3,300 per family member) in-network and $9,900 ($4,950 per family member) out-of-network.
Out-of-Pocket Maximum
The most you will pay during the plan year before your insurance pays 100%.
Individual out-of-pocket max is $4,250 in-network and $12,750 out-of-network.
Family out-of-pocket max is $8,500 ($4,250 per family member) in-network and $25,500 ($12,750 per family member) out-of-network.

tables.tableDescription: Bi-weekly associate payroll contributions for HDHP coverage
Comprehensive coverage details for HDHP plan - HSA eligible
Prescription costs are determined by the tier assigned to each specific prescription drug.

rows.label: Tier one
cells: $5/$15 copay in-network, 40% up to $250 out-of-network (after deductible)

rows.label: Tier two
cells: $40 copay in-network, 40% up to $250 out-of-network (after deductible)

rows.label: Tier three
cells: $60 copay in-network, 40% up to $250 out-of-network (after deductible)

rows.label: Speciality
cells: 30% up to $250 in-network, 40% up to $250 out-of-network (after deductible)

rows.label: Speciality
cells: 30% up to $250 in-network, not covered out-of-network

benefitChapters.title: Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
benefitChapters.description: The Team Member Assistance Program (EAP) through Mutual of Omaha provides confidential mental, emotional, and physical health support for team members and their families. Services include licensed pro

children.text: The Team Member Assistance Program (EAP) through Mutual of Omaha provides confidential mental, emotional, and physical health support for team members and their families. Services include licensed professionals for counseling with no cost regardless of medical plan enrollment, 24-hour phone access, and helpful resources online.
Services available 24/7 by phone and online at www.mutualofomaha.com/eap.
Overview

The Team Member Assistance Program (EAP) through Mutual of Omaha provides confidential mental, emotional, and physical health support for team members and their families. Services include licensed professionals for counseling with no cost regardless of medical plan enrollment, 24-hour phone access, and helpful resources online.
Free Visits
A designated number of face-to-face visits per issue with a licensed professional, no cost.
Services include:
Emotional health and wellbeing
Job pressures
Stress, anxiety, depression
Grief and loss
Alcohol or drug dependency
Marriage or family problems
Financial or legal advice

benefitChapters.title: Health Savings Account (HSA) & Flexible Spending Account (FSA)
benefitChapters.description: HSA and FSA account options for tax-advantaged savings.

children.text: Understand your tax-advantaged savings account options.
What is a Health Savings Account?
Your HSA can be used for qualified medical expenses for you, your legal spouse/domestic partner, and tax dependents.
Use your Anthem Blue Cross Visa debit card to pay for qualifying expenses with no need for reimbursement submissions.
Eligible expenses include doctor's visits, eye exams, prescriptions, laser eye surgery, PPE, over-the-counter meds, menstrual products, and more as per IRS Publication 502.
Benefits of HSA
HSA funds grow tax-free and roll over year to year without expiration.
HSA funds are portable and owned by you, with no vesting or forfeiture provisions.
You can save funds for healthcare expenses or retirement, tax-free.
Who is eligible for an HSA?
You must be enrolled in an HSA-eligible High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP).
You cannot be covered by your legal spouse/domestic partner’s or parent’s non-HDHP plan.
You must not have a Healthcare FSA or Health Reimbursement Account.
You must not be eligible to be claimed as a dependent on another's tax return.
You must not be enrolled in Medicare or TRICARE.
You must not have received VA medical benefits for non-service-related care in the past 90 days.
How to manage your HSA account
Enroll by selecting the HDHP plan and submitting HSA enrollment materials and contribution amounts.
Premier America Credit Union establishes your HSA account and verifies your bank information.
You control your contributions and when you use funds or reimburse yourself.
Eligible expenses
Qualified medical expenses for you and your family as defined by IRS Publication 502.
What is a Flexible Spending Account?

A Flexible Spending Account (FSA) is a special tax-free account you put money into to pay for certain out-of-pocket expenses not covered by your insurance. FSAs reduce your taxable income and increase take-home pay.
Understanding different types of FSAs
You cannot have both an HSA and Healthcare FSA simultaneously.
Funds in FSAs are 'use it or lose it' but there is a 2.5 months grace period to incur additional expenses after plan year ends.
Dependent Care FSA allows pre-tax funds to pay for childcare or eldercare expenses while you work or attend school.
Termination allows 90 days post-termination to submit claims.

tables.tableTitle: Premier America Credit Union HSA Employer Contribution

cells: Available if you elect other coverage; cannot have an HSA.

cells: Used for medical expenses (deductibles, copays, coinsurance, menstrual products, PPE).
Used for costs of caring for elder or child dependents.

benefitChapters.title: Survivor Benefits
benefitChapters.description: Premier America Credit Union provides Basic Life and Accidental Death & Dismemberment (AD&D) insurance to team members automatically, covering $50,000 in benefits via Mutual of Omaha.

children.text: Premier America Credit Union provides Basic Life and Accidental Death & Dismemberment (AD&D) insurance to team members automatically, covering $50,000 in benefits via Mutual of Omaha.
RS&H provides Basic Life and Accidental Death & Dismemberment Insurance (AD&D)
Premier America Credit Union provides Basic Life and Accidental Death & Dismemberment (AD&D) insurance to team members automatically, covering $50,000 in benefits via Mutual of Omaha.
Coverage amount
Basic Life and AD&D benefit is $50,000.
Eligible team members automatically receive basic coverage.
Additional voluntary life insurance available for purchase in increments up to $500,000 depending on coverage type.
AD&D Coverage
Basic coverage includes Accidental Death & Dismemberment insurance through Mutual of Omaha providing financial protection to your survivors.
Benefit included in Basic Life insurance.
Additional voluntary life and AD&D insurance options available to purchase with payroll deduction.
Other Voluntary benefits
Voluntary benefits include additional voluntary life insurance for team members, spouses, and dependent children, with coverage amounts and requirements described.
Voluntary life insurance purchased in increments up to specified maximums.
Proof of good health required above certain coverage levels.
Dependent child coverage available up to $10,000.
Payroll deduction funds premiums.
Designating Beneficiaries
Name a primary and contingent beneficiary with full details. Benefits to minors are held until they reach age 18. Contact HR or legal counsel with questions.

benefitChapters.title: Supplemental Health
benefitChapters.description: Supplemental health benefits provided by Allstate.

children.text: Provider: Allstate
What is Supplemental Health?
Supplemental health benefits supplement medical plan coverage to help cover unexpected costs.
Coverage includes accident, critical illness, and hospital indemnity insurance.
Offered at discounted group rates through Allstate.
Critical Illness Coverage
Pays lump-sum benefit upon diagnosis of covered conditions.
Benefit amounts: $15,000 or $30,000 for team member, 100% for spouse, 50% for children (no additional cost).
Guaranteed issue with no medical questions.
Wellness benefit payable once per calendar year ($50).
Covered illness include:
Benign brain tumor
Carcinoma in situ
Coma
Coronary disease
Heart attack
Life threatening cancer
Loss of hearing
Loss of sight
Loss of speech
Major organ failure
Paralysis
Skin cancer
Stroke
Payment frequency
Wellness benefit payable once per person, per calendar year.
Critical illness benefits payable based on date of diagnosis/event.
Accident Coverage
Accident coverage provides benefits for expenses related to accidents outside of work.
Helps pay deductibles, copays, and everyday expenses such as mortgage or car payments.
Benefits payable directly to you to use as you wish.
Notes & Disclaimers
This is a fixed indemnity policy, not health insurance.
The policy pays a limited dollar amount even if medical bills are higher.
It is not a substitute for comprehensive health insurance and does not include all federal consumer protections.

rows.label: Major Diagnostic Exam (CT or CAT Scans, MRI, PET Scan, EEG, SPECT, Joint Imaging, DTI Scan, MRA Scan) Within 60 Days of the Accident
Medical Mobility Devices (cane, crutches, knee walker, walking boot, other)

benefitChapters.title: Income Protection (Disability)
benefitChapters.description: Short-term and long-term disability insurance and income protection benefits.

children.text: Premier America Credit Union offers Short-Term Disability (STD) and Long-Term Disability (LTD) insurance to protect your income if you cannot work due to injury or illness.
Understand your disability insurance options.
Overview
Premier America Credit Union offers Short-Term Disability (STD) and Long-Term Disability (LTD) insurance to protect your income if you cannot work due to injury or illness.
Short-Term Disability Insurance
Replaces 55% of your income if disabled short-term.
Weekly maximum benefit is $1,075.
Elimination period: Day 1 for accidents/injury, Day 8 for illness.
Maximum benefit period: 90 days.
Basic Long-Term Disability (LTD) Insurance
Replaces 50% of your income if disabled long-term.
Monthly maximum benefit: $6,000.
Elimination period: 90 days.
Benefits last as long as you are disabled or until Social Security Normal Retirement Age.
Notes & Disclaimers
You may choose to have the company pay for LTD coverage, making benefits taxable upon receipt, or pay premiums post-tax to receive untaxed benefits.

benefitChapters.title: Additional Voluntary Benefits
benefitChapters.description: Premier America Credit Union offers various additional voluntary benefits to support your life, including pet insurance, identity protection, and a 529 College Savings Plan.

children.text: Premier America Credit Union offers various additional voluntary benefits to support your life, including pet insurance, identity protection, and a 529 College Savings Plan.
Provides coverage for veterinary expenses related to accidents and illnesses including exams, medications, and surgeries for dogs, cats, birds, reptiles, and exotic pets.

**How it works**
Optional wellness coverage reimburses preventive care for dogs and cats. Enrollment and information available through Nationwide.

Includes X-rays, medications, vet visits, surgeries, hospital stays

**How it works**

Data and device protection
Identity theft insurance
Fraud restoration services

**How it works**
